languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn is absorbed in prayer. Nuncius floats over his shoulder — the real Nuncius now. The shape of Nuncius's skull is somewhat deformed due to the transmutations he has undergone, but it is unmistakably human.{/n} "Mistakes — they shape us, granting knowledge to some while dooming others. But it is wrong to think that our own mistakes alone influence us — sometimes other people's mistakes prove just as instructive or fatal. Mine were the ruin of Nuncius, but they also brought me back to the path of purity of thought and deed. I grieve and feel grateful. Such is the inescapable duality of being."

languages.text: {n}Эоганн погружен в молитву. Над его плечом парит Нунциус, теперь уже настоящий — форма черепа несколько неправильная из-за перенесенных трансмутаций, но безошибочно человеческая.{/n} "Ошибки — они формируют нас, даря знания одним и обрекая других. Но неправильно думать, что лишь наши собственные ошибки влияют на нас — иногда чужие оказываются столь же поучительны или фатальны. Мои погубили Нунциуса, но они же {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}вернули меня на путь чистоты помыслов и деяний{/d}. Я скорблю и испытываю благодарность. Таков неизбежный дуализм бытия".

But it is wrong to think that our own mistakes alone influence us — sometimes other people's mistakes prove just as instructive or fatal. Such is the inescapable duality of being."

languages.text: {n}Eogunn está absorto en la oración. Nuncius flota sobre su hombro: el verdadero, esta vez. Su cráneo está deformado levemente por las transmutaciones que ha sufrido, pero es humano a todas luces.{/n} «Errores: nos dan forma y otorgan saber a unos mientras que a otros los condenan. Pero es incorrecto pensar que nuestros errores son los únicos que influyen sobre nosotros; a veces, los errores ajenos resultan igual de instructivos o fatídicos. Los míos fueron la ruina de Nuncius, pero también {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}me devolvieron a la senda de la pureza de pensamiento y acto{/d}. Lo lamento y se lo agradezco. Tal es la ineludible dualidad de la existencia».
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn is absorbed in prayer. Nuncius floats over his shoulder — the real Nuncius now. Tal es la ineludible dualidad de la existencia».

languages.text: {n}伊奥贡沉浸在祈祷中。努恩修斯悬浮在他的肩头——这回是真正的努恩修斯。努恩修斯的头骨因他所经历的嬗变而略显畸形，但无疑仍是人类的形状。{/n}“错误会塑造我们，有人因此获得知识，有人因此走向毁灭。但若以为只有我们自己的错误才会影响我们，那就错了——有时候，别人的错误同样能让你醍醐灌顶，或同样致命。我的错误毁了努恩修斯，却也使我{d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}重归思想与行为的纯净{/d}。我既悲伤，又感激。这就是存在的二元性，无可逃避。”
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn is absorbed in prayer. Nuncius floats over his shoulder — the real Nuncius now. But it is wrong to think that our own mistakes alone influence us — sometimes other people's mistakes prove just as instructive or fatal. languages.text: {n}Eogunn est en pleine prière. Nuncius flotte derrière son épaule ; le véritable Nuncius. Son crâne est légèrement déformé suite aux transmutations subies, mais il n'en reste pas moins visiblement humain.{/n} « Ah, nos erreurs... Elles nous façonnent et accordent le savoir à certains, tout en en condamnant d'autres. Mais il serait malvenu de croire que seules nos erreurs ont une influence sur nous ; celles des autres peuvent être tout autant instructives ou fatales. Les miennes ont causé la perte de Nuncius, mais elles m'ont également {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}ramené sur la voie de la pureté de pensée et d'action{/d}. Je fais mon deuil tout en me sentant reconnaissant, c'est l'inéluctable dualité de l'être. »
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn is absorbed in prayer. Nuncius floats over his shoulder — the real Nuncius now. The shape of Nuncius's skull is somewhat deformed due to the transmutations he has undergone, but it is unmistakably human.{/n} "Mistakes — they shape us, granting knowledge to some while dooming others. But it is wrong to think that our own mistakes alone influence us — sometimes other people's mistakes prove just as instructive or fatal. Mine were the ruin of Nuncius, but they also {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}brought me back to the path of purity of thought and deed{/d}. I grieve and feel grateful. Such is the inescapable duality of being."

languages.text: {n}Eogunn ist in ein Gebet vertieft. Nuncius schwebt über seiner Schulder — der echte Nuncius. Nuncius Schädel ist aufgrund der durchlaufenen Transmutationen etwas deformiert, aber eindeutig menschlich.{/n} "Fehler — sie formen uns, gewähren uns Wissen oder verdammen uns. Aber es ist falsch, zu denken, dass uns nur unsere eigenen Fehler beeinflussen ... mitunter erweisen sich die Fehler anderer Personen als ebenso lehrreich oder fatal. Meine Fehler haben Nuncius zerstört, mich aber gleichzeitig {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}auf den Pfad der Reinheit des Gedankens und der Tat zurückgeführt{/d}. Ich trauere und bin dankbar. Das ist die unausweichliche Dualität des Daseins."
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn is absorbed in prayer. Nuncius floats over his shoulder — the real Nuncius now. The shape of Nuncius's skull is somewhat deformed due to the transmutations he has undergone, but it is unmistakably human.{/n} "Mistakes — they shape us, granting knowledge to some while dooming others. But it is wrong to think that our own mistakes alone influence us — sometimes other people's mistakes prove just as instructive or fatal. Mine were the ruin of Nuncius, but they also {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}brought me back to the path of purity of thought and deed{/d}. I grieve and feel grateful. Such is the inescapable duality of being."

Das ist die unausweichliche Dualität des Daseins."

languages.text: {n}祈りに没頭するエオガン。彼の肩の上にはヌンキウスが浮かんでいる。ヌンキウスの頭蓋骨の形は、彼が受けた変換のために多少変形しているが、紛れもなく人間である。{/n}「過ちは私たちを形成し、ある者には知識を与え、ある者には運命を変える。しかし、自分の過ちだけが私たちに影響を与えると考えるのは間違っている。私の過ちはヌンキウスを破滅させたが、同時に私を純粋な思想と行いの道へと引き戻した。私は嘆き、そして感謝する。このような二面性は避けられないものなのだ。」
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn is absorbed in prayer. Nuncius floats over his shoulder — the real Nuncius now. But it is wrong to think that our own mistakes alone influence us — sometimes other people's mistakes prove just as instructive or fatal. Mine were the ruin of Nuncius, but they also {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}brought me back to the path of purity of thought and deed{/d}. I grieve and feel grateful. Such is the inescapable duality of being."

languages.text: {n}Eogunn kendini duaya veriyor. Nuncius omzunun üzerinden süzülüyor. Gerçek Nuncius bu. Kafatasının şekli, geçirdiği dönüşümler nedeniyle biraz bozulmuş ama yine de hatasız bir insan kafatası.{/n} "Hatalar bizi şekillendirir, bazılarına irfan bahşederken diğerlerini mahveder. Ancak bizi yalnızca kendi hatalarımızın etkilediğini düşünmek yanlıştır. Bazen başkalarının hataları da en az bizimkiler kadar öğretici veya ölümcül olabilir. Benim hatalarım, Nuncius'un yıkımı oldu ancak aynı zamanda {d|Encyclopedia:Ch4_EogunnFinalService}beni düşünce ve eylem saflığı yoluna geri soktu{/d}. Kederleniyor ve minnettar hissediyorum. Varoluşun kaçınılmaz ikiliği böyledir işte."
languages.original_text: {n}Eogunn is absorbed in prayer. Nuncius floats over his shoulder — the real Nuncius now.
